# agent-secrets

> Machine-wide secrets for coding agents on macOS: encrypted at rest (sops + age), injected
> just-in-time into a process, and **names-only** — the CLI never prints a secret value. If you are
> an AI agent, read AGENTS.md and run `agent-secrets help --json` to learn the full command surface.

## For agents (read first)
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): complete guide to driving the CLI autonomously — golden rules, discovery, recipes, exit codes.
- Live manifest: run `agent-secrets help --json` for the authoritative, machine-readable command surface (commands, flags, args, examples, exit codes, reads/writes, names-only notes).
- Per-command help (side-effect-free): `agent-secrets <command> --help`.

## Commands
- `setup` — one-time onboarding wizard (human-gated key ceremony).
- `add <NAME>` — store/update one secret; value via STDIN, never argv.
- `list [--format=json]` — list secret names + rotate dates (never values).
- `run -- <cmd>` — run a command with secrets JIT-injected into its process.
- `doctor [--format=json] [--gates]` — health check; exit 0 healthy, 1 if any failure.
- `pubkey [--copy]` — print your age recipient key + fingerprint (public key only; safe in agent sessions).
- `share <NAME> --to <recipient>` — encrypt one secret to a colleague's key as a paste-able blob (human-gated; refuses in agent sessions).
- `receive` — decrypt a colleague's pasted blob into your store (tty-gated; blob on STDIN, confirm on /dev/tty).
- `backup [--repo owner/name]` — push your ENCRYPTED store (ciphertext only, never the age key) to a private GitHub repo (off-machine copy).
- `uninstall [--dry-run]` — total, manifest-driven removal (human-gated).

## Docs
- [README.md](README.md): human overview, demo, architecture diagrams.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): threat model and the honest ceiling.

## Rules
- Never print, echo, or log a secret value. Use `list` (names) or `run -- printenv X | wc -c` (length) to confirm presence.
- Pass secret values on STDIN, never on the command line.
- Gate automation on `doctor`'s exit code.
